##NAMES=Anapaite
##RRUFFID=R141081
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Fe^2+^(PO_4_)_2_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Messel mine, Messel, Darmstadt, Hesse, Germany
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Rock Currier
##DESCRIPTION=Pale green to colorless subparallel aggregates. Associated with messelite (R140175) and pyrite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141081
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_1.95_Mg_0.05_)(Fe_0.99_Mg_0.02_)(P_1.00_O_4_)_2_·4H_2_O ; The grey portion is anapaite and the bright portion is pyrite.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
